content.problemStatement:
Given an integer **N**, Calculate and print the **sum** and **product** of its digit.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single positive integer NNN <= 109.


Output Format

Print the sum and product of digits of NNN on single line with a space between them.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print two integers separated by a space: the sum of the digits of N and the product of the digits of N. Print each test case result on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only test case has N = 22. Digits are 2 and 2. Sum = 2+2 = 4, Product = 2*2 = 4.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate over the decimal representation of N digit by digit. While extracting the last digit using n % 10 we add it to a running sum and multiply it into a running product. After processing a digit we remove it with n /= 10. The loop stops when n becomes 0. The algorithm is O(number of digits) per test case, which is at most 10 for the given constraints, and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log10 N) ≈ O(T) because N ≤ 10^9 has at most 10 digits.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long n; if(scanf("%lld",&n)!=1) break; long long sum=0, prod=1; if(n==0){ sum=0; prod=0; } else { while(n){ int d=n%10; sum+=d; prod*=d; n/=10; } } printf("%lld %lld\n",sum,prod);} return 0; }
code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        n=int(next(it))
        s=0
        p=1
        if n==0:
            s=0
            p=0
        else:
            while n:
                d=n%10
                s+=d
                p*=d
                n//=10
        out.append(f"{s} {p}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__=="__main__":
    solve()
